heading: Weight-bumping in household goods transportation
label: 14912 Weight-bumping in household goods transportation
notes: Prior ProvisionsProvisions similar to those in this section were contained in prior to the general amendment of this subtitle by .section 11917 of this titlePub. L. 104–88, § 102(a)
Effective DateSection effective , except as otherwise provided in , see , set out as a note under .Jan. 1, 1996Pub. L. 104–88section 2 of Pub. L. 104–88section 1301 of this title
amendments: Prior ProvisionsProvisions similar to those in this section were contained in prior to the general amendment of this subtitle by .section 11917 of this titlePub. L. 104–88, § 102(a)

children.heading: .—Weight-Bumping Defined
children.label: a .—Weight-Bumping Defined
children.text: For the purposes of this section, “weight-bumping” means the knowing and willful making or securing of a fraudulent weight on a shipment of household goods which is subject to jurisdiction under subchapter I or III of chapter 135.

children.heading: .—Penalty
children.label: b .—Penalty
children.text: Whoever has been found to have committed weight-bumping shall be fined under title 18 or imprisoned not more than 2 years, or both.
